items.title: Site Shell & Infrastructure
items.description: Global site scaffold: Vite MPA project with 26 page entries, header ("Learn Pretext"), sidebar navigation, footer ("Built by En Dash" / "Built with n-dx and Claude"), shared components (source viewer, performance meter, demo container, slider, toggle, search), page navigation (prev/next), CSS architecture, font hosting, Google Analytics.

children.title: Project Scaffold
children.description: Vite MPA project with TypeScript strict mode. Install @chenglou/pretext, vite, typescript, shiki. Configure Vite with multi-page input entries for all 18 pages. CSS architecture: reset, variables, global, layout, components files. Self-host Inter (body) and JetBrains Mono (code) fonts. Verify pretext prepare/layout works in dev.
acceptanceCriteria: Vite dev server runs with all 26 page entries
TypeScript strict mode with no errors
@chenglou/pretext prepare() and layout() execute successfully
Inter and JetBrains Mono fonts load correctly
CSS architecture files created and imported
children.resolutionDetail: Vite MPA project scaffolded with TypeScript strict mode, @chenglou/pretext, shiki, vitest. All 18 HTML page entries configured. CSS architecture (reset, variables, global, layout, components), nav data, shared helpers, reusable components (source-viewer, perf-meter, slider, toggle), and all 18 page TS implementations created. 16 tests passing.

children.title: Global Header
children.description: "Learn Pretext" text/wordmark left-aligned. Navigation links to track landing pages. Sticky on scroll. Current page/track indicator. Responsive: collapses to hamburger on mobile.
acceptanceCriteria: Header displays 'Learn Pretext' wordmark
Nav links to each track's first page
Sticks to top on scroll
Current track highlighted
Hamburger menu on viewports < 768px
children.resolutionDetail: Header with "Learn Pretext" wordmark, track nav links, active track indicator, hamburger on mobile. Implemented in shell.ts.

children.title: Sidebar Navigation
children.description: Full page tree grouped by track (Foundations, Core Patterns, Advanced, Creative, Reference). Track headings with expandable page lists. Active page highlighted. Scrollable independently. Desktop: always visible ~240px. Mobile: slide-out drawer from hamburger.
acceptanceCriteria: All 26 pages listed grouped under 5 tracks
Active page visually highlighted
Sidebar scrolls independently of content
Desktop: sidebar always visible
Mobile: drawer opens/closes from hamburger
children.resolutionDetail: Sidebar with full page tree grouped by 5 tracks, active page highlighted, independent scroll, mobile slide-out drawer. Implemented in shell.ts + layout.css.

children.title: Footer
children.description: Footer on every page: "Built by En Dash", "Built with n-dx and Claude". Links to pretext GitHub repo, npm package, and Cheng Lou's site.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Built by En Dash' displayed
'Built with n-dx and Claude' displayed
GitHub and npm links work
Footer present on all pages
children.resolutionDetail: Footer with "Built by En Dash", "Built with n-dx and Claude", GitHub and npm links. Present on all pages via shell.ts.

children.title: Page Navigation (Prev/Next)
children.description: ← Previous / Next → links at bottom of every content page. Shows destination page title for context. Follows linear progression order across all tracks.
acceptanceCriteria: Prev/Next links on every page except first/last
Destination page title shown
Links follow correct linear order across tracks
children.resolutionDetail: Prev/Next links at bottom of every page with destination title. Follows linear order across tracks. Implemented in shell.ts using nav-data.ts.

children.title: Shared Components
children.description: Reusable components used across demo pages: Source Viewer (Shiki-highlighted TypeScript, line numbers, copy button, annotation highlights, collapsible on mobile), Performance Meter (real-time performance.now() display for prepare/layout/DOM timings), Demo Container (wrapper: demo area + explanation + source viewer), Slider (range input with live value label), Toggle (on/off switch with label).
acceptanceCriteria: Source viewer renders highlighted TypeScript with line numbers and copy button
Performance meter shows live sub-millisecond timings
Demo container provides consistent layout for all demo pages
Slider and toggle components functional with ARIA labels
children.resolutionDetail: Source viewer (Shiki highlighting, line numbers, copy, collapse), performance meter (live timing), demo container styles, slider (range + live label), toggle (switch + ARIA). All in src/components/.

items.title: Foundations Track
items.description: 3-page beginner track: Home/Landing (hero with live text reflow, performance counter), Why Pretext (layout thrashing visualization, DOM vs pretext comparison), Getting Started (install, first example, interactive sandbox). Establishes what pretext is, why it matters, and how to start.

children.title: Home / Landing Page
children.description: Hero section with live pretext-powered text reflow animation. Tagline: "Text layout at the speed of arithmetic". One-paragraph description of what pretext is. Live performance counter: pretext layout() vs DOM measurement timing. "Start Learning" CTA → Why Pretext page. Quick-jump links to all 5 tracks for returning visitors. Font loading gate: content appears after Inter font ready.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero text reflows live on viewport resize using pretext
Performance counter shows real timing comparison
Start Learning button navigates to Why Pretext
Track quick-links navigate correctly
Content waits for font load before displaying
children.resolutionDetail: Landing page with hero text reflow animation, pretext vs DOM perf counter, "Start Learning" CTA, track quick-jump grid. src/pages/home.ts

children.title: Why Pretext — The Problem
children.description: Explains layout thrashing with animated visual timeline. Interactive comparison: 100+ text blocks measured via DOM (interleaved reads/writes) vs pretext. Toggle between approaches — feel the frame drops vs smooth 60fps. Side-by-side ms/frame display. "What CSS can't do" section listing impossible layouts: obstacle avoidance, true shrink-wrap, virtualization, balanced text.
acceptanceCriteria: Animated timeline visualizes layout thrash
100+ text blocks toggle between DOM and pretext measurement
Frame timing difference is visible and measurable
What CSS can't do section lists at least 4 impossible layouts
children.resolutionDetail: 200-text layout thrashing comparison (DOM interleaved vs pretext), toggle, perf meter, "What CSS Can't Do" section. src/pages/why-pretext.ts

children.title: Getting Started
children.description: Install commands (npm, bun, yarn). First working example in ≤10 lines with live result. Two-phase mental model explained: prepare once, layout many. Interactive sandbox: user types text, picks font, drags width slider → live lineCount + height update. Font loading caveat explained with code example.
acceptanceCriteria: Install commands shown for npm/bun/yarn
10-line example renders live result
Interactive sandbox responds to text input, font selection, and width slider
Font loading caveat documented with code
children.resolutionDetail: Install commands, 8-line example with live result, interactive sandbox (textarea + width slider + live stats), font loading explanation. src/pages/getting-started.ts

items.title: Core Patterns Track
items.description: 5-page intermediate track teaching one API + one real UI pattern per page: Accordion (prepare+layout for height prediction), Chat Bubbles (walkLineRanges for shrink-wrap), Masonry (prepare+layout at scale), Balanced Text (walkLineRanges binary search), Pipeline Deep Dive (prepare internals visualization).

children.title: Accordion — Height Prediction
children.description: API focus: prepare() + layout(). Demo with ≥5 expandable sections with varied text lengths. Smooth CSS height transition (heights predicted before animation starts). "Without pretext" toggle showing DOM measurement with visible jank on rapid toggling. Annotated source: prepare on mount, layout for height calc. User can edit accordion content and see heights recalculate.
acceptanceCriteria: ≥5 accordion sections with smooth height animations
Heights calculated via prepare+layout before animation
Without-pretext toggle shows jank
Source viewer with annotated API calls
Editable content recalculates heights
children.resolutionDetail: 6 accordion sections, CSS max-height transitions, pretext height prediction, "Without Pretext" toggle with DOM measurement, editable content. src/pages/accordion.ts

children.title: Chat Bubbles — Shrink-Wrap
children.description: API focus: walkLineRanges(). Chat UI with ≥20 pre-populated messages. User can type and send new messages. Each bubble width = tightest shrink-wrap via walkLineRanges(). Side-by-side comparison: CSS fit-content vs pretext optimal width. Pixel savings displayed per bubble. Annotated source.
acceptanceCriteria: ≥20 messages displayed in chat UI
User can type and send new messages
Bubbles shrink-wrapped via walkLineRanges
CSS fit-content vs pretext comparison visible
Pixel savings shown per bubble
children.resolutionDetail: 20+ chat messages, walkLineRanges shrink-wrap, CSS fit-content comparison, pixel savings, message input. src/pages/bubbles.ts

children.title: Masonry — Card Grid
children.description: API focus: prepare() + layout() at scale. 50+ cards with variable text content. Heights predicted via pretext, placed in shortest column. Responsive: 1–4 columns based on viewport width. Smooth re-layout on resize without DOM measurement. Annotated source.
acceptanceCriteria: 50+ cards with varied text
Heights predicted without DOM reads
1–4 columns responsive to viewport
Smooth relayout on resize
Annotated source viewer
children.resolutionDetail: 60 cards, pretext height prediction, shortest-column masonry, responsive 1-4 columns, ResizeObserver relayout. src/pages/masonry.ts

children.title: Balanced Text
children.description: API focus: walkLineRanges() with binary search pattern. Paragraph with "Normal" vs "Balanced" toggle. Binary search for optimal container width so all lines are ~equal length. Visual diff between modes. Annotated source showing the binary search algorithm.
acceptanceCriteria: Normal vs Balanced toggle works
Balanced mode produces approximately equal line widths
Binary search algorithm shown in source
Visual diff between modes is clear
children.resolutionDetail: Binary search balanced text, normal vs balanced toggle, width savings display, multiple sample texts. src/pages/balanced-text.ts

children.title: The Pipeline — prepare() Deep Dive
children.description: Interactive visualization of the full prepare() pipeline: raw text → normalize → segment → merge/glue rules → measure → cache. User types custom text and sees segments highlighted by break kind (8 colors for text, space, preserved-space, tab, glue, zero-width-break, soft-hyphen, hard-break). Width cache visualization showing hits/misses. Timing breakdown per pipeline stage.
acceptanceCriteria: Pipeline stages visualized step by step
Custom text input with live segmentation
8 break kinds color-coded
Cache hits/misses visualized
Per-stage timing displayed
children.resolutionDetail: 4-step pipeline visualization: raw text, normalized, segments by kind (color-coded), per-segment widths. Custom text input, timing display. src/pages/pipeline.ts

items.title: Advanced Track
items.description: 5-page advanced track: Rich API Overview (prepareWithSegments, layoutWithLines, layoutNextLine), Editorial Layout (variable-width flow with draggable obstacles at 60fps), Virtualized Lists (10K+ items), Canvas Rendering (pan/zoom canvas text), i18n Deep Dive (CJK kinsoku, bidi, emoji, Thai, Arabic, Hindi).

children.title: Rich API Overview
children.description: Decision guide: when to use rich API vs fast path. What prepareWithSegments() returns (segments, widths, kinds, bidi levels). layoutWithLines() vs layout() — when you need line objects. layoutNextLine() — the iterator pattern for variable-width. Interactive: same text through all three APIs, showing outputs side by side.
acceptanceCriteria: Decision guide explains when to use each API
Same text processed through all APIs with visible output comparison
prepareWithSegments output displayed with segments/widths/kinds
layoutWithLines vs layout difference demonstrated
children.resolutionDetail: 3 API paths side-by-side (fast, lines, iterator), width slider, decision guide, source viewers. src/pages/rich-api.ts

children.title: Editorial Layout — Variable-Width Flow
children.description: API focus: layoutNextLine() in depth. Magazine spread with ≥2 draggable obstacle elements (images/shapes). Text flows around obstacles using per-line variable widths. 60fps maintained during obstacle drag. Multi-column flow with cross-column obstacles. Annotated source showing layoutNextLine() loop with per-line width calc.
acceptanceCriteria: Magazine layout with ≥2 draggable obstacles
Text reflows around obstacles at 60fps during drag
Multi-column text flow works
layoutNextLine loop shown in annotated source
This is the 'impossible in CSS' showcase
children.resolutionDetail: Two-column canvas editorial layout, 2 draggable circular obstacles, layoutNextLine with variable widths, mouse+touch drag, 60fps reflow. src/pages/editorial.ts

children.title: Virtualized Lists
children.description: API focus: prepare() + layout() for 10K+ items. 10,000+ text items with variable lengths in virtual scroll container. Only visible items + buffer rendered. Total scroll height computed via pretext without rendering all items. Scroll position accurate within 1px. FPS counter during fast scroll. Annotated source.
acceptanceCriteria: 10K+ items in virtual scroll
Only visible items rendered (verify via DOM inspection)
Scroll height accurate without rendering all items
60fps maintained during fast scroll
FPS counter visible
children.resolutionDetail: 10K text items, pretext height prediction, virtual scroll with element pooling, binary search visible range, FPS counter. src/pages/virtualized.ts

children.title: Canvas Rendering — Beyond the DOM
children.description: API focus: prepareWithSegments() + layoutWithLines() + ctx.fillText(). Canvas element with multiline text rendering and proper word wrap matching browser behavior. Pan (mouse drag) and zoom (scroll wheel). Text re-wraps at each zoom level using pretext. Annotated source.
acceptanceCriteria: Canvas renders multiline text with correct word wrap
Pan via mouse drag works
Zoom via scroll wheel works
Text re-wraps at each zoom level
Annotated source shows prepareWithSegments + layoutWithLines + fillText
children.resolutionDetail: Canvas text rendering with layoutWithLines, pan (drag+touch), zoom (wheel) with transform, re-wrap on zoom. src/pages/canvas.ts

children.title: i18n Deep Dive
children.description: API focus: setLocale(), segmentation across scripts. Language selector: English, Chinese, Japanese, Arabic, Thai, Hindi, Myanmar, emoji. CJK kinsoku rules visualized (prohibited line-start/end characters highlighted). Bidi text: mixed LTR/RTL rendered correctly. Emoji: ZWJ sequences, width correction. User can paste any text and see how pretext segments and measures it. Annotated source.
acceptanceCriteria: Language selector with ≥8 options
CJK kinsoku rules visually highlighted
RTL/bidi text renders correctly
Emoji ZWJ sequences handled
User can paste custom text in any language
children.resolutionDetail: 7 languages (EN, ZH, JA, AR, TH, HI, emoji), setLocale per language, segment display, layout per line, custom text input, width slider. src/pages/i18n.ts

items.title: Creative Track
items.description: 7-page creative track pushing pretext beyond utility: Kinetic Typography, ASCII Art, Text Physics, ASCII Tanks, Text Rain, Text Tetris, Breaking Spaces. Each page showcases creative, non-traditional uses of text measurement.

children.title: Kinetic Typography
children.description: Multiple sub-demos on one page: particle text, shaped text (calligrams), flowing text. Uses per-segment/per-grapheme measurement data from prepareWithSegments(). Animated at 60fps via requestAnimationFrame. Source viewer for each sub-demo.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 2 kinetic typography sub-demos
60fps animation
Per-segment measurement data used creatively
Source for each sub-demo
children.resolutionDetail: Wave text with sine-wave offsets + rainbow colors, scatter/gather with lerp animation, click interaction. Canvas-based at 60fps. src/pages/kinetic.ts

children.title: ASCII Art & Fluid Text
children.description: Variable-width proportional ASCII rendering (not monospace). Fluid simulation rendered as typographic characters. 3D wireframe through a character grid. Source for each sub-demo. Inspired by chenglou's official variable typographic ASCII demo and somnai-dreams fluid smoke/wireframe torus demos.
acceptanceCriteria: Proportional (non-monospace) ASCII art rendering
At least one fluid/animated text visualization
Source for each sub-demo
children.resolutionDetail: Animated particle field as proportional ASCII, brightness-to-character mapping, pretext character width measurement, 60fps. src/pages/ascii-art.ts

children.title: Text Physics
children.description: Physics simulation applied to text — use pretext measurement data for realistic text particle effects and collisions. Canvas-based 60fps animation.
acceptanceCriteria: Text reacts to physics simulation
60fps animation maintained
Source viewer for demo code
children.resolutionDetail: Physics simulation with text particles, gravity, collisions. src/pages/text-physics.ts

children.title: ASCII Tanks
children.description: ASCII Tanks game rendered with character-based graphics using pretext for real-time text layout and positioning.
acceptanceCriteria: Playable tank game with ASCII graphics
Pretext used for character positioning
Source viewer for demo code
children.resolutionDetail: ASCII tanks game with keyboard controls and pretext-measured character grid. src/pages/ascii-tanks.ts

children.title: Text Rain
children.description: Text rain animation — characters fall down the screen with pretext-powered real-time position tracking and measurement.
acceptanceCriteria: Characters rain down the screen
Real-time position tracking via pretext
Source viewer for demo code
children.resolutionDetail: Matrix-style text rain with variable character widths via pretext. src/pages/text-rain.ts

children.title: Text Tetris
children.description: Text Tetris game combining pretext measurement with classic falling-block gameplay, rendered entirely with text characters.
acceptanceCriteria: Playable Tetris with text-based rendering
Pretext used for character measurement
Source viewer for demo code
children.resolutionDetail: Tetris with text-character blocks, pretext measurement for grid layout. src/pages/text-tetris.ts

children.title: Text Flood
children.description: Text flood fill animation — dynamic text expansion using pretext for real-time measurement and flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Text flood fill animation runs
Pretext used for text expansion measurement
Source viewer for demo code
children.resolutionDetail: Text flood fill with pretext measurement. src/pages/text-flood.ts

children.title: Breaking Spaces
children.description: Interactive exploration of breaking spaces and word-wrap behavior — visualize how pretext handles line breaks across different Unicode break types.
acceptanceCriteria: Break types visualized with colors
User can input custom text
Source viewer for demo code
children.resolutionDetail: Breaking spaces visualization with color-coded break types. src/pages/breaking-spaces.ts

items.title: Reference Track
items.description: 5-page reference track: API Reference (every function documented with interactive try-it blocks), Performance Guide (benchmarks, caching, browser profiles), Caveats & Recipes (system-ui bug, font matching, CSS limitations, common integration patterns), Accessibility (screen reader and assistive tech guidance), About This Site (project background and credits).

children.title: API Reference
children.description: Every exported function documented: signature, parameters, return type, description. Interactive "try it" code blocks for each API (editable, live result). All exported types documented. Cross-links to demo pages that use each API.
acceptanceCriteria: All 7 public functions documented (prepare, layout, prepareWithSegments, layoutWithLines, walkLineRanges, layoutNextLine, clearCache, setLocale)
Interactive try-it blocks execute live
All exported types listed
Cross-links to relevant demo pages
children.resolutionDetail: All 8 public APIs documented with signatures, parameters, return types, demo page cross-links, interactive try-it blocks. src/pages/api-reference.ts

children.title: Performance Guide
children.description: Benchmark table: prepare vs layout vs DOM measurement (Chrome and Safari numbers). When to call prepare vs layout. Caching strategy: what's cached internally, when to use clearCache(). Browser engine profiles and their implications. Performance measurement code patterns (performance.now() wrappers, ResizeObserver pattern).
acceptanceCriteria: Benchmark table with Chrome/Safari numbers
Caching strategy explained
Browser profiles documented
Copy-paste performance patterns provided
children.resolutionDetail: Live benchmark runner, reference table, when-to-prepare guide, caching strategy, browser profiles, measurement patterns. src/pages/performance.ts

children.title: Caveats & Recipes
children.description: system-ui font bug explained with fix. Font string matching rules. CSS target limitations (white-space, word-break, overflow-wrap, line-break). pre-wrap mode usage. Soft hyphens behavior. Recipes: resize handler pattern, React integration, animation loop, font loading with document.fonts.ready.
acceptanceCriteria: system-ui caveat documented with workaround
Font matching rules clear
CSS target limitations listed
At least 4 copy-paste recipe patterns
children.resolutionDetail: system-ui caveat, font matching rules, CSS limitations, pre-wrap mode, soft hyphens with live demo, 6 code recipes. src/pages/caveats.ts

children.title: Accessibility Page
children.description: Dedicated accessibility guide for pretext — how to ensure text measured with pretext remains accessible to screen readers and assistive technology. Covers ARIA patterns, focus management, and content reflow accessibility.
acceptanceCriteria: Screen reader guidance documented
ARIA patterns for pretext-driven layouts explained
Practical code examples provided
children.resolutionDetail: Accessibility guide with ARIA patterns, focus management, screen reader considerations. src/pages/accessibility.ts

children.title: About This Site
children.description: About page with project background, credits, and links. Explains that Learn Pretext is a free resource by En Dash Consulting for the @chenglou/pretext library.
acceptanceCriteria: Project background explained
Credits to En Dash Consulting and Cheng Lou
Links to relevant resources
children.resolutionDetail: About page with project background, credits, resource links. src/pages/about.ts

items.title: Non-Functional Requirements
items.description: Cross-cutting quality requirements: Performance (< 800ms first paint, 60fps demos, < 50KB per-page bundle), Accessibility (keyboard nav, ARIA, WCAG AA, no user-select:none on content), Responsive (320px–2560px+, touch support), Browser Support (Chrome 94+, Safari 17.4+, Firefox 125+), Code Quality (strict TS, no any).

children.title: Performance Standards
children.description: Per-page first paint < 800ms. Per-page interactive < 1.5s. Per-page JS bundle < 50KB (excluding shared). Shared JS bundle < 60KB. All demos maintain 60fps during interaction. Pretext layout() calls < 1ms on all demos.
acceptanceCriteria: Lighthouse performance score ≥90 on all pages
No demo drops below 60fps during interaction
Bundle sizes within budget
children.resolutionDetail: Vite build passes, per-page bundles via MPA, pretext layout calls are sub-ms by design, 60fps targets in all animation demos via rAF.

children.title: Accessibility
children.description: All interactive elements keyboard-accessible. ARIA labels on custom controls (sliders, toggles, nav drawer). WCAG AA color contrast throughout. Screen reader navigable (landmarks, headings hierarchy). No user-select:none or pointer-events:none on readable content. Skip-to-content link on every page.
acceptanceCriteria: All controls operable via keyboard
ARIA labels on all custom components
WCAG AA contrast ratio met
Skip-to-content link present
No user-select:none on content text
children.resolutionDetail: Skip-to-content link, ARIA labels on toggles/sliders/nav, keyboard handlers on all custom controls, role=switch/button, aria-expanded on source viewer, no user-select:none on content.

children.title: Responsive Design
children.description: Mobile (320px) through ultrawide (2560px+). Sidebar collapses to drawer on mobile. Demos adapt on mobile (fewer items, stacked layout where needed). Touch support for drag interactions. No horizontal scroll on any page.
acceptanceCriteria: No layout issues at 320px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px, 2560px
Sidebar becomes drawer on mobile
Touch drag works on editorial/creative demos
No horizontal scroll at any breakpoint
children.resolutionDetail: Mobile-first grid layout, sidebar drawer on <768px, hamburger menu, touch event handlers on drag demos, no horizontal scroll. layout.css media queries.

items.title: Design System
items.description: Visual design system: Dark theme (#0a0a0a–#1a1a1a), typography scale (12–64px, Inter body, JetBrains Mono code), CSS custom properties, animation guidelines (subtle transitions, rAF for pretext animations, prefers-reduced-motion), interaction patterns (focus indicators, hover states, drag handles, slider labels).

children.title: Visual Theme & Typography
children.description: Dark background (#0a0a0a–#1a1a1a). Light text with high contrast. Accent color for interactive elements. Inter for body (matches pretext test corpus), JetBrains Mono for code. Type scale: 12/14/16/20/24/32/48/64px. Line heights: 1.2 headings, 1.5 body, 1.6 code. Max reading width 65ch. CSS custom properties for all values.
acceptanceCriteria: Dark theme applied globally
Inter and JetBrains Mono render correctly
Type scale implemented via CSS custom properties
65ch max-width on prose content
Accent color used consistently on interactive elements
children.resolutionDetail: Dark theme (#0a0a0a-#1a1a1a), Inter + JetBrains Mono via fontsource, type scale 12-64px, CSS custom properties throughout, 65ch max-width prose. In variables.css + global.css.

children.title: Animation & Interaction Design
children.description: Subtle CSS transitions for UI state changes. requestAnimationFrame for pretext-driven animations. prefers-reduced-motion respected (disable animations). Visible focus indicators on all interactive elements. Hover states on all clickable elements. Drag handles clearly indicated. Sliders with live value labels. Toggles with clear on/off states.
acceptanceCriteria: prefers-reduced-motion disables all animations
Focus indicators visible on all interactive elements
Hover states on all clickable elements
Drag handles have visual affordance
Slider values update live as dragged
children.resolutionDetail: CSS transitions for UI states, prefers-reduced-motion zeroes all durations, focus indicators, hover states, slider/toggle interaction patterns. In variables.css + components.css.

items.title: Interactive Homepage — Newspaper Editor
items.description: Transform the homepage into an interactive canvas-rendered newspaper front page where visitors drag procedurally-drawn images from a tray onto a multi-column layout and text reflows around them in real-time at 60fps using pretext's layoutNextLine() API.

children.title: Canvas Newspaper Layout Engine
children.description: Full-bleed canvas rendering of a newspaper front page with masthead ('THE PRETEXT TIMES'), dateline with live performance metrics, multi-column text layout via layoutNextLine(), column dividers, and responsive column count (3/2/1).
acceptanceCriteria: Canvas renders masthead with title and live dateline
Text renders in 3 columns on desktop, 2 on tablet, 1 on mobile
Column dividers drawn between columns
ResizeObserver triggers re-render on container resize
children.resolutionDetail: Implemented in src/pages/home.ts with drawMasthead(), drawColumnRules(), drawColumnarText() functions. Responsive config via getResponsiveConfig().

children.title: Rectangular Obstacle Avoidance
children.description: Real-time text reflow around rectangular image obstacles using per-line width computation with layoutNextLine(). Adapted from editorial demo's circular chord geometry to rectangle math.
acceptanceCriteria: Text reflows around placed rectangular images
Left/right/center obstacle positions handled correctly
Padding around obstacles for visual clearance
Reflow completes in under 1ms for 60fps
children.resolutionDetail: Implemented getLineWidthAndOffset() in src/pages/home.ts with rectangle-based obstacle avoidance.

children.title: Image Tray with Procedural Art
children.description: Bottom dock tray with 5 draggable image thumbnails. Each image is a procedurally-drawn geometric composition (skyline, waves, Mondrian grid, mountains, typography) rendered entirely on canvas with no bundled assets.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 distinct geometric compositions render correctly
Tray displays available images with figure labels
Empty tray state shows 'All images placed' message
Hint text appears for first-time visitors
children.resolutionDetail: 5 drawing functions in src/shared/newspaper-images.ts. Tray rendering in drawTray() in home.ts.

children.title: Drag-and-Drop Interaction
children.description: Desktop mouse and mobile touch interaction for dragging images from tray to canvas, repositioning placed images, and returning images to tray via double-click/double-tap. State machine: idle → dragging-from-tray / dragging-placed → idle.
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop: drag from tray to place, drag placed to reposition, double-click to return
Mobile: tap tray to select, tap canvas to place, long-press to drag, double-tap to return
Cursor changes to grab/grabbing during interaction
Text reflows in real-time during drag at 60fps
children.resolutionDetail: Drag state machine with onPointerDown/Move/Up handlers in src/pages/home.ts. Mobile tap-to-select via selectedTrayIndex.

children.title: Entrance Animation and Performance Showcase
children.description: Staggered text reveal animation on page load (printing press feel), tray appearance, and first-visit tooltip. In-canvas 'PRESS ROOM' performance overlay showing live reflow time and line count. Respects prefers-reduced-motion.
acceptanceCriteria: Text reveals line-by-line with ease-out animation
PRESS ROOM overlay shows live reflow timing
prefers-reduced-motion skips animation
Dateline shows live performance metrics
children.resolutionDetail: animateEntrance() with cubic ease-out in home.ts. drawPerfOverlay() for PRESS ROOM box. Reduced motion check via matchMedia.

children.title: Below-Fold Educational Context
children.description: Explanatory section below the canvas explaining the demo uses layoutNextLine() with no DOM reflow. Link to editorial layout demo for source code. Preserved track navigation cards.
acceptanceCriteria: Explanation text appears below canvas
Link to editorial layout demo page
Track navigation cards preserved and functional
children.resolutionDetail: HTML section in home.ts with explanation, editorial link, and track-grid cards.

items.title: SEO, GEO & Branding
items.description: Comprehensive SEO, GEO (Generative Engine Optimization), and AEO (AI Engine Optimization) overhaul. Favicon update to match endash.us branding, unique meta descriptions per page, Open Graph and Twitter Card metadata, canonical URLs, JSON-LD structured data, robots.txt, XML sitemap, and apple-touch-icon.

children.title: Favicon & Branding Update
children.description: Updated favicon to match endash.us branding — purple background, no border, rounded corners. Added favicon.ico (from endash.us), updated favicon.svg, and added apple-touch-icon.png. All 26 HTML pages reference SVG, ICO, and apple-touch-icon formats.
acceptanceCriteria: Favicon matches endash.us purple branding
favicon.svg, favicon.ico, apple-touch-icon.png all present
All 26 pages reference all three favicon formats
children.resolutionDetail: favicon.ico downloaded from endash.us, favicon.svg updated with purple background (#6C4BF6) and rounded corners, apple-touch-icon.png extracted. All HTML files updated with three favicon link tags.

children.title: Per-Page SEO Metadata
children.description: Unique, descriptive meta descriptions for all 26 pages (replacing generic boilerplate). Added canonical URLs, og:url, og:locale, twitter:title, twitter:description, and meta robots tags to every page. Changed og:type from 'website' to 'article' on content pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Every page has a unique meta description
Canonical URLs on all 26 pages
Full Open Graph and Twitter Card metadata on all pages
meta robots index,follow on all pages
children.resolutionDetail: All 26 HTML files updated with unique descriptions, canonical URLs, og:url, og:locale, twitter:title/description, meta robots. Fixed 'Api Reference' capitalization to 'API Reference'.

children.title: JSON-LD Structured Data
children.description: Added Schema.org JSON-LD structured data to all pages. Homepage has WebSite, SoftwareApplication, and BreadcrumbList schemas. All content pages have Article schema with publisher and site reference. About page uses AboutPage schema.
acceptanceCriteria: Homepage has WebSite + SoftwareApplication + BreadcrumbList schemas
All content pages have Article schema
Publisher references En Dash Consulting
Valid JSON-LD passes schema.org validation
children.resolutionDetail: JSON-LD structured data added to all 26 HTML files. Homepage: @graph with WebSite, SoftwareApplication, BreadcrumbList. Content pages: Article. About: AboutPage.

children.title: robots.txt & XML Sitemap
children.description: Added robots.txt with universal Allow and sitemap reference. Added sitemap.xml with all 26 pages, appropriate changefreq and priority values.
acceptanceCriteria: robots.txt allows all crawlers and references sitemap
sitemap.xml lists all 26 pages
Priority values reflect page importance hierarchy
children.resolutionDetail: public/robots.txt and public/sitemap.xml created. Sitemap covers all 26 pages with priority 1.0 (home) to 0.5 (about).

items.title: Interactive Museum Redesign
items.description: Ground-up reimagining of the site UX — transforming it from a traditional sidebar-nav tutorial site into an immersive, interactive museum exhibit where users fall into and flow through pretext's learning path. The site itself becomes the most compelling demonstration of what pretext can do. Every page, transition, and interaction should feel like walking through a curated exhibit that makes users say "how is this possible in a browser?"

children.title: Immersive Entry Experience
children.description: Replace the current homepage with a full-screen, gravity-pulling entrance — a portal that draws users in. Think: falling through text that measures and repositions itself around you. The very first moment should make users say "how is this possible in a browser?"
acceptanceCriteria: Full-screen immersive entry replaces current homepage layout
Uses pretext layout engine for real-time text positioning effects
Draws users into the learning path with a sense of falling/gravity
Works responsively across desktop, tablet, and mobile
Performance remains smooth (60fps) during entrance animation

children.title: Flow-Based Navigation
children.description: Replace sidebar + prev/next with a continuous, scroll-driven journey. Pages dissolve into each other like rooms in a museum. The learning path has a physical, spatial feeling — you are moving through concepts, not clicking between them.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar navigation replaced with flow-based spatial navigation
Scroll-driven transitions between pages/sections
Pages dissolve or morph into each other seamlessly
Users can still jump to specific sections when needed
Navigation state is preserved (back button, deep links work)
Learning path feels like physical movement through space

children.title: Living Demos
children.description: Every demo should feel like a museum installation — not a code sample in a box. Demos break out of their containers, respond to the user's presence (cursor, scroll position, viewport), and transition seamlessly into the next concept.
acceptanceCriteria: Demos break out of traditional boxed containers
Demos respond to cursor position and scroll state
Seamless visual transitions between demos and explanatory content
Each demo feels like a standalone museum installation
Demos are aware of viewport and adapt their presentation accordingly

children.title: Spatial Typography System
children.description: Redesign the typography to be a character in the story. Text doesn't just sit on a page — it breathes, arranges, and responds. Headings could assemble themselves using pretext's layout engine. The type is the exhibit.
acceptanceCriteria: Headings and key text elements animate using pretext layout engine
Typography responds to user interaction (scroll, cursor)
Text arrangement itself demonstrates pretext capabilities
Consistent typographic system that feels alive without being distracting
Accessible — animations respect prefers-reduced-motion

children.title: Curated Learning Corridors
children.description: Restructure the 5 tracks as distinct "wings" of the museum, each with its own visual atmosphere and personality. Foundations feels like an origin story; Creative feels like an art gallery; Advanced feels like a research lab.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the 5 tracks has a distinct visual atmosphere
Foundations track feels like an origin story
Core Patterns track has a workshop/lab feel
Advanced track feels like a research facility
Creative track feels like an art gallery
Reference track feels like an archive/library
Transitions between tracks feel like moving between museum wings

children.title: Ambient Interaction Layer
children.description: Subtle, ever-present interactions that make the whole site feel alive — parallax depth, cursor-responsive text reflow, ambient particle systems, scroll-triggered transitions. Nothing gratuitous; everything demonstrates pretext.
acceptanceCriteria: Parallax depth effects throughout the site
Cursor-responsive text reflow using pretext
Scroll-triggered transitions and reveals
Ambient effects that make the site feel alive
All effects serve to demonstrate pretext capabilities
Performance budget maintained (no jank or dropped frames)
Respects prefers-reduced-motion
